element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Fundamental combat against weakest CPU opponents
Early offensive technique against scaling difficulty
Core mechanic for body slams, piledrivers, throws
High-risk offensive positioning against mid-tier opponents
Strategic retreating and environmental awareness required for survival
Positioning advantage becomes critical in later matches as CPU pins become faster
Risk/reward of additional attacks vs. guaranteed pinfall
First major difficulty spike; requires mastery of all basic and intermediate mechanics

element_introduction_order.element: Title defense sequences (2 matches per opponent)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Extended endurance testing against all roster members with accumulated knowledge
Extreme difficulty; considered one of most difficult NES boss characters

typical_relief_triggers: Exiting ring to break referee count
Using turnbuckle attacks for distance reset
Match victory screen and ranking advancement
typical_tension_triggers: Player character downed and in vulnerable position
Pinfall attempt with referee repositioning time
CPU opponent climbing turnbuckle for aerial attack
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Escalating difficulty across sequential matches; early matches (1-3) feature forgiving CPU with clear timing windows; mid-matches (4-5 leading to King Slender) introduce faster grapple responses and combo chains; championship defense phase cycles through all opponents with accumulated aggression; final Great Puma fight represents sustained high-pressure encounter requiring frame-perfect defensive positioning

difficulty_curve.early_game: Matches 1-3 feature CPU opponents with significant recovery windows and predictable grappling patterns; punching/kicking loops effective; player learning phase for lock-up mechanics
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Matches 4-5 before King Slender; CPU begins chaining attacks faster; turnbuckle usage becomes necessary; timing on pinfall escapes tightens; first mechanical mastery check
difficulty_curve.late_game: Championship defense phase (10 title matches) cycles through all opponents sequentially with accumulated aggression; opponents remember player tactics; endurance becomes factor; cumulative fatigue affects input precision
notable_spikes: King Slender championship bout after 5 wins - first major difficulty wall requiring complete mechanical mastery
Great Puma inter-promotional final boss - documented as one of most difficult NES boss encounters; extreme aggression and unpredictable grapple follow-ups

notable_level_patterns.description: Single-player mode presents fixed opponent roster in scaling difficulty order; player fights each of five potential challengers once, then King Slender, then defends title by fighting each opponent twice more. Creates rhythm of familiar opponent matchups with increasing aggression on rematches.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Matches 1-5 (initial run) vs. Championship defense matches 6-15 (rematches with same roster)

notable_level_patterns.description: Two-stage progression: reach championship (requires 5 wins), then maintain championship (requires 10 successful defenses). Each stage presents different psychological pressure; initial climb vs. defensive endurance test.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: King Slender match marks gate; Great Puma match represents second gate

notable_level_patterns.description: Two-player matches use best-of-three-falls structure without time limit, contrasting single-player five-minute matches. Extended engagement allows for momentum shifts and comeback mechanics.
